steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: “This idea is known as the service recovery ______” is an independent clause (subject idea, verb is known).
After the blank: “research suggests that it has important implications for customer loyalty and retention” is an independent clause (subject research, verb suggests).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
When two independent clauses need to be joined, the rule requires either a period, a semicolon, or a comma plus a coordinating conjunction.

Choice C (paradox, and) applies the comma-plus-conjunction pattern correctly.

Those choices violate the rule—A creates a comma splice by omitting the conjunction, B creates a fused sentence by omitting both punctuation and conjunction—while D violates it by placing the comma after the conjunction instead of before.

That confirms C is correct.
